reasoning.typography: Inter Display + Tiempos Text is literally the AI-era stencil pairing. Safe, readable, zero character. Could be any SaaS product launched 2022-2024.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headline dominates adequately but the product screenshot below competes for attention. Tab navigation fragments focus. Not clear what I should look at first in the lower half.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Swap 'Attio' for any B2B CRM name and this works identically. 'Revenue platform engineered for scale' + customer logos + product UI screenshot = maximum genericness. No editorial stance.
reasoning.color: Restrained black/white base with minimal accent usage. The product UI shows purple accents judiciously. Clean discipline, though perhaps too safe/corporate.
reasoning.density: Generic uniform padding everywhere. The hero area has appropriate breathing room, but the navigation sidebar screenshot feels like filler rather than confident content. Nothing bold about the spacing choices.
reasoning.voice: IA is pure SaaS stencil: Hero value prop → Product tabs (Ask Attio, Data model, Workflows, Reporting) → Customer logos → CTAs. Zero reflection of actual story or personality. Could be generated.

winning_patterns: Clean color restraint with disciplined black/white base
Readable hierarchy in hero headline area
Navigation sidebar UI preview attempts to show product depth
Customer logo bar shows legitimate credibility
anti_patterns: Inter Display + Tiempos Text = the 2023 AI stencil combo everyone uses
Generic SaaS hero formula: big claim + subhead + dual CTA buttons
Tab navigation (Ask Attio/Data model/Workflows/Reporting) feels like feature checklist
Product screenshot 'Thinking...' placeholder text feels unfinished/lazy
No editorial voice or perspective—pure capability listing
Uniform padding throughout lacks spatial confidence
